title: phlox speciosa f. lanceolata
intro: Phlox speciosa f. lanceolata, also known as the western or mountain phlox, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Phlox speciosa f. lanceolata include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its essential oil can be extracted for topical use.
